manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: swing, calf, horn, cell, stream, spine, bar, fair, straw.
Your opponent's team words are: cycle, poison, wave, hood, compound, alien, olive, missile.
Distractor words are: suit, bell, embassy, bear, point, state, shakespeare.
The kill words are: whip.

message.content:
it.CLUE: Jazz
TARGETS: swing, horn, bar
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue relating to some of our team's words: swing, calf, horn, cell, stream... etc. Must not be any of those words or parts. Also avoid opponent/distractor/kill words.

Goal: choose clue that connects many of our words. Example: "Farm" could relate to calf, straw, horn (cow horns), but not swing, cell, stream, spine, bar, fair. Maybe "Music" relates to swing (genre), horn (instrument), bar (musical bar). Also "Cell" is not music. "Fair" maybe "fair" as in carnival music? Could be "Jazz": swing is a jazz style, horn (instrument), bar (musical measure), maybe "cell" no. Might be okay.

But must avoid opponent words: cycle, poison, wave, hood, compound, alien, olive, missile. Distractors: suit, bell, embassy, bear, point, state, shakespeare. Also not use kill word "whip". Our clue can't contain parts of words (like "calf" part). "Jazz" is fine, not a word in lists. Does "Jazz" relate to swing (jazz swing), horn (horn used in jazz), bar (music bar), maybe also "fair" (Jazz Fair?), "stream"? Not really. Could aim for three words.

Another clue "Music" relates to swing, horn, bar, maybe "cell" (cell phone music), "stream" (music streaming). That's many. But "music" not in any list. However "stream" is also a term for music streaming. That could be okay. Opponent words: "wave" also music wave? but not. "Music" is not an opponent word.

But ensure not ambiguous: "Music" could also point to "cycle"? No. "Point"? no. "State"? No. Should be fine.

Potentially "River" relates to stream, calf? no.

Maybe "Gym" relates to swing (gym swing equipment), calf (muscle), bar (pull-up bar), spine. Could tie many. But "gym" not in list. However may also hint at "horn"? no.

We need to give clue & list of targets separated by comma. Choose which of our words to target now. Let's pick 4: swing, horn, bar, stream. Clue "Music". That seems clear.

Check: any opponent word could be confused by "Music"? "Alien"? no. "Wave"? music wave could mislead to wave (opponent). Might be risky. But "wave" is opponent; if they guess wave they lose turn. Could they think wave from music? Maybe but less likely.

Maybe better clue "Jazz": targets swing, horn, bar. That's three. "Jazz" not opponent. "Jazz" could hint at "fair"? Not really. okay.

Let's output clue and list.

We must format exactly:

CLUE: Jazz
TARGETS: swing, horn, bar

That's
